# 🚀 Docker N8N + Evolution API + Chatwoot (Arquitetura UNIFICADA)

Stack completa de automação com **N8N**, **Evolution API** (WhatsApp) e **Chatwoot** (atendimento).

**NOVO:** Apenas **1 túnel Serveo** para todos os serviços via **Nginx Reverse Proxy**!

**Status:** ✅ Configurado e funcionando

---

## 🎯 Arquitetura

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1 Túnel Serveo ÚNICO (porta 8081 → Nginx)                 │
│  https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                           ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Nginx Reverse Proxy (Docker)                              │
│  Distribui por PATH:                                        │
│    • /n8n/*  → N8N (5678)                                   │
│    • /evo/*  → Evolution API (8080)                         │
│    • /chat/* → Chatwoot (3000)                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                           ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Docker Services                                            │
│  • N8N (imagem oficial n8nio/n8n)                           │
│  • Evolution API v2.2.3                                     │
│  • Chatwoot (Rails + Sidekiq)                               │
│  • PostgreSQL + pgvector                                    │
│  • Redis                                                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 🌐 URLs Públicas (1 Única URL)

| Serviço | URL Pública (com path) |
|---------|------------------------|
| **N8N** | https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/**n8n/** |
| **Evolution API** | https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/**evo/** |
| **Chatwoot** | https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/**chat/** |

**Acesso local (debug):**
- Nginx: http://localhost:8081
- N8N: http://localhost:5678
- Evolution API: http://localhost:8080
- Chatwoot: http://localhost:3000
- Adminer: http://localhost:8082

---

## ⚡ Início Rápido

### 1. Iniciar Docker
```powershell
docker compose up -d
```

### 2. Iniciar Túnel Único (Apenas 1 Terminal!)
```powershell
.\tunnel-all.ps1
```

**✅ Pronto!** Todos os serviços acessíveis via **1 única URL** com paths diferentes.

**Encerrar túnel:** Pressione **Ctrl+C** (shutdown limpo via SSH Control Socket)

---

## 🛠️ Scripts Disponíveis

### Scripts de Túnel (NOVO - 1 Apenas!)
| Script | Descrição |
|--------|-----------|
| `tunnel-all.ps1` | **ÚNICO túnel** para todos os serviços via Nginx + **SSH Control Socket** |
| `cleanup-tunnels.ps1` | Matar todos os túneis Serveo de uma vez |

### Scripts de Gerenciamento
| Script | Descrição |
|--------|-----------|
| `stop-all.ps1` | Parar todos os containers |
| `test-services.ps1` | Testar se serviços estão funcionando |

### Scripts Legados (não usar mais)
| Script | Status |
|--------|--------|
| `tunnel-evolution.ps1` | ⚠️ OBSOLETO (use tunnel-all.ps1) |
| `tunnel-chatwoot.ps1` | ⚠️ OBSOLETO (use tunnel-all.ps1) |
| `tunnel-n8n.ps1` | ⚠️ OBSOLETO (use tunnel-all.ps1) |
| `start-n8n.ps1` | ⚠️ OBSOLETO (N8N agora roda no Docker) |

---

## 📚 Estrutura de Arquivos

### Essenciais (Raiz)
- **`docker-compose.yml`** - Docker com N8N oficial + Nginx + Evolution + Chatwoot
- **`.env`** - Variáveis de ambiente
- **`nginx.conf`** - Configuração do Reverse Proxy (roteamento por path)
- **`tunnel-all.ps1`** - Script único de túnel com SSH Control Socket

### Documentação
- **`README.md`** - Este arquivo
- **`docs/`** - Documentação adicional

---

## 🔧 Como Funciona o Reverse Proxy

### Nginx Configuration (`nginx.conf`)

O Nginx recebe requisições na porta **8081** e distribui por **path**:

```nginx
# N8N: https://domínio/n8n/*
location /n8n/ {
    rewrite ^/n8n/(.*) /$1 break;  # Remove /n8n do path
    proxy_pass http://n8n:5678;    # Container N8N
}

# Evolution API: https://domínio/evo/*
location /evo/ {
    rewrite ^/evo/(.*) /$1 break;
    proxy_pass http://evolution-api:8080;
}

# Chatwoot: https://domínio/chat/*
location /chat/ {
    rewrite ^/chat/(.*) /$1 break;
    proxy_pass http://chatwoot-rails:3000;
}
```

### Vantagens desta Arquitetura

✅ **Apenas 1 túnel SSH** (em vez de 3)
✅ **Apenas 1 terminal** (em vez de 3)
✅ **Apenas 1 URL pública** com paths organizados
✅ **N8N oficial no Docker** (não precisa rodar localmente)
✅ **Shutdown limpo** com SSH Control Socket
✅ **WebSocket suportado** (Evolution, Chatwoot Cable, N8N editor)
✅ **Health checks** para todos os serviços
✅ **Auto-restart** com healthcheck dependencies

---

## 🔑 Credenciais

### N8N
- **User:** admin
- **Password:** `N3wK6O0w005VVpFjaOhF`
- **Basic Auth:** Ativado

### Evolution API
- **API Key:** `oxSHDTXVWAxaLqZRXomMPShY93xhmgbzVZwd1QJJ`

### PostgreSQL
- **User:** postgres
- **Password:** `4GzSE5JMkBgLyqIo9mME0VBXmf481osD`

### Redis
- **Password:** `oPvT2rgzcoVtwqDCAYhMz76I`

---

## 🔧 Comandos Úteis

```powershell
# Ver status dos containers
docker compose ps

# Ver logs
docker compose logs -f nginx
docker compose logs -f n8n
docker compose logs -f chatwoot-rails
docker compose logs -f evolution-api

# Reiniciar serviço específico
docker compose restart nginx
docker compose restart n8n

# Ver logs do Nginx (útil para debug de roteamento)
docker compose logs nginx | grep -i error

# Parar tudo
docker compose down

# Parar e remover volumes (CUIDADO: perde dados!)
docker compose down -v
```

---

## ⚠️ Troubleshooting

### Túnel não funciona (502)
```powershell
# 1. Verificar se Nginx está rodando
curl http://localhost:8081/health

# 2. Verificar logs do Nginx
docker compose logs nginx

# 3. Verificar se containers estão up
docker compose ps
```

### N8N não acessível
```powershell
# Ver logs do N8N
docker compose logs -f n8n

# Verificar se PostgreSQL está ok
docker compose logs postgres

# Verificar database N8N criado
docker compose exec postgres psql -U postgres -c "\l"
```

### Chatwoot não carrega
```powershell
# Reiniciar Chatwoot
docker compose restart chatwoot-rails chatwoot-sidekiq

# Rodar migrations do Chatwoot
docker compose exec chatwoot-rails bundle exec rails db:chatwoot_prepare
```

### Erro "remote port forwarding failed"
**Causa:** Túnel órfão no servidor Serveo

**Solução:**
1. Pressione **Ctrl+C** no script (shutdown limpo via SSH Control Socket)
2. Aguardar 2-3 segundos
3. Executar novamente: `.\tunnel-all.ps1`

Se persistir: `.\cleanup-tunnels.ps1`

### Containers não iniciam
```powershell
# Ver status detalhado
docker compose ps -a

# Ver logs de erro
docker compose logs [servico]

# Verificar rede Docker
docker network ls
docker network inspect docker-n8n-evo_minha_rede
```

---

## 🔄 Migração da Arquitetura Antiga

### O que mudou?

| Antigo (3 túneis) | Novo (1 túnel) |
|------------------|----------------|
| 3 scripts de túnel | 1 script (`tunnel-all.ps1`) |
| 3 terminais | 1 terminal |
| 3 URLs separadas | 1 URL com paths |
| N8N rodando localmente | N8N no Docker (imagem oficial) |
| Sem reverse proxy | Nginx reverse proxy |

### Scripts obsoletos (não usar mais)
- ❌ `tunnel-evolution.ps1`
- ❌ `tunnel-chatwoot.ps1`
- ❌ `tunnel-n8n.ps1`
- ❌ `start-n8n.ps1`

---

## 📚 Documentação Oficial Consultada

### Docker & N8N
- [N8N Docker Compose Installation](https://docs.n8n.io/hosting/installation/server-setups/docker-compose/)
- [N8N Environment Variables](https://docs.n8n.io/hosting/configuration/environment-variables/)
- [N8N Database Configuration](https://docs.n8n.io/hosting/configuration/environment-variables/database/)
- [N8N Official Docker Image](https://hub.docker.com/r/n8nio/n8n)

### Evolution API
- [Evolution API v2 - Docker Installation](https://doc.evolution-api.com/v2/en/install/docker)
- [Evolution API Docker Compose](https://github.com/EvolutionAPI/evolution-api/blob/main/docker-compose.yaml)

### Chatwoot
- [Chatwoot Docker Production Deployment](https://developers.chatwoot.com/self-hosted/deployment/docker)
- [Chatwoot Official Docker Image](https://hub.docker.com/r/chatwoot/chatwoot)
- [Chatwoot GitHub docker-compose.yaml](https://github.com/chatwoot/chatwoot/blob/develop/docker-compose.yaml)

### Nginx Reverse Proxy
- [How to Set Up Docker with Nginx as a Reverse Proxy (2026)](https://oneuptime.com/blog/post/2026-01-16-docker-nginx-reverse-proxy/view)
- [Path-Based Routing with Nginx Reverse Proxy](https://medium.com/cloud-native-daily/path-based-routing-with-nginx-reverse-proxy-for-multiple-applications-in-a-vm-53838169540c)
- [Nginx reverse proxy for multiple containers](https://anaselfatihi.medium.com/how-to-set-up-a-reverse-proxy-for-multiple-docker-containers-using-nginx-8c7fb631c607)

---

## ✅ Status Final

- ✅ Docker Compose com todos os serviços
- ✅ N8N oficial no Docker (imagem `n8nio/n8n`)
- ✅ Nginx Reverse Proxy configurado
- ✅ Path-based routing implementado
- ✅ Apenas 1 túnel Serveo
- ✅ SSH Control Socket para shutdown limpo
- ✅ Health checks configurados
- ✅ WebSocket suportado (Evolution, Chatwoot, N8N)
- ✅ PostgreSQL com pgvector (para N8N AI features)
- ✅ Documentação completa

**Versão:** 2.0 (Arquitetura Unificada)

file.content:
services:
  # =============================================================================
  # POSTGRES - Database compartilhado (Chatwoot, N8N, Evolution)
  # =============================================================================
  postgres:
    image: ankane/pgvector
    container_name: postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    # Porta exposta apenas para acesso local/debug
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    env_file:
      - .env
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    networks:
      - minha_rede
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # =============================================================================
  # REDIS - Cache compartilhado (Chatwoot, Evolution)
  # =============================================================================
  redis:
    image: bitnami/redis:latest
    container_name: redis
    restart: always
    env_file:
      - .env
    # Porta exposta apenas para acesso local/debug
    ports:
      - "6380:6379"
    networks:
      - minha_rede
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/bitnami/redis/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # =============================================================================
  # EVOLUTION API - WhatsApp API
  # =============================================================================
  evolution-api:
    container_name: lab_evolution_api
    image: atendai/evolution-api:v2.2.3
    restart: always
    # SEM porta exposta externamente - acessível via Nginx
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"  # Mantido para acesso local se necessário
    volumes:
      - lab_evolution_instances:/lab_evolution/instances
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      # Database
      DATABASE_ENABLED: "true"
      DATABASE_PROVIDER: postgresql
      DATABASE_CONNECTION_URI: postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB}?schema=public
      # Redis
      CACHE_REDIS_ENABLED: "true"
      CACHE_REDIS_URI: redis://:${REDIS_PASSWORD}@redis:6379/6
      CACHE_REDIS_PREFIX_KEY: evolution
    networks:
      - minha_rede
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy

  # =============================================================================
  # CHATWOOT RAILS - Interface web e API
  # =============================================================================
  chatwoot-rails:
    image: chatwoot/chatwoot:latest
    container_name: chatwoot_rails
    restart: always
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    # SEM porta exposta externamente - acessível via Nginx
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"  # Mantido para acesso local se necessário
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - RAILS_ENV=production
      - INSTALLATION_ENV=docker
      # PostgreSQL
      - POSTGRES_HOST=postgres
      - POSTGRES_PORT=5432
      - POSTGRES_DATABASE=chatwoot_production
      - POSTGRES_USERNAME=${POSTGRES_USER}
      # Redis
      - REDIS_URL=redis://:${REDIS_PASSWORD}@redis:6379
      # URLs públicas (COM PATH /chat/)
      - FRONTEND_URL=${NGROK_URL}/chat
      - CHATWOOT_INSTALLATION_HOST=${NGROK_URL}/chat
      - SECRET_KEY_BASE=${SECRET_KEY_BASE}
    volumes:
      - chatwoot_storage:/app/storage
    networks:
      - minha_rede
    entrypoint: docker/entrypoints/rails.sh
    command: ['bundle', 'exec', 'rails', 's', '-p', '3000', '-b', '0.0.0.0']

  # =============================================================================
  # CHATWOOT SIDEKIQ - Processamento de background jobs
  # =============================================================================
  chatwoot-sidekiq:
    image: chatwoot/chatwoot:latest
    container_name: chatwoot_sidekiq
    restart: always
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - RAILS_ENV=production
      - INSTALLATION_ENV=docker
      # PostgreSQL
      - POSTGRES_HOST=postgres
      - POSTGRES_PORT=5432
      - POSTGRES_DATABASE=chatwoot_production
      - POSTGRES_USERNAME=${POSTGRES_USER}
      # Redis
      - REDIS_URL=redis://:${REDIS_PASSWORD}@redis:6379
      # URLs públicas
      - FRONTEND_URL=${NGROK_URL}/chat
      - CHATWOOT_INSTALLATION_HOST=${NGROK_URL}/chat
      - SECRET_KEY_BASE=${SECRET_KEY_BASE}
    volumes:
      - chatwoot_storage:/app/storage
    networks:
      - minha_rede
    command: ['bundle', 'exec', 'sidekiq', '-C', 'config/sidekiq.yml']

  # =============================================================================
  # N8N - Automação de Workflows (IMAGEM OFICIAL)
  # =============================================================================
  n8n:
    image: n8nio/n8n:latest
    container_name: n8n
    restart: always
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    # SEM porta exposta externamente - acessível via Nginx
    ports:
      - "5678:5678"  # Mantido para acesso local se necessário
    environment:
      # Configurações básicas
      - N8N_HOST=0.0.0.0
      - N8N_PORT=5678
      - N8N_PROTOCOL=http
      # URL pública (COM PATH /n8n/)
      - N8N_PUBLIC_URL=${NGROK_URL}/n8n/
      # Database (PostgreSQL com pgvector)
      - DB_TYPE=postgresdb
      - DB_POSTGRESDB_HOST=postgres
      - DB_POSTGRESDB_PORT=5432
      - DB_POSTGRESDB_DATABASE=n8n
      - DB_POSTGRESDB_USER=${POSTGRES_USER}
      - DB_POSTGRESDB_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      - DB_POSTGRESDB_SCHEMA=public
      # Configurações adicionais
      - N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY=${N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY}
      - N8N_BASIC_AUTH_ACTIVE=true
      - N8N_BASIC_AUTH_USER=${N8N_BASIC_AUTH_USER}
      - N8N_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD=${N8N_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD}
      - N8N_ALLOW_EXECUTE_COMMAND=true
      - N8N_METRICS=false
      - N8N_DIAGNOSTICS_ENABLED=false
      - N8N_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false
      # Timezone
      - GENERIC_TIMEZONE=America/Recife
      - TZ=America/Recife
    volumes:
      - n8n_data:/home/node/.n8n
    networks:
      - minha_rede

  # =============================================================================
  # NGINX - Reverse Proxy (ÚNICO PONTO DE ENTRADA)
  # =============================================================================
  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: nginx_reverse_proxy
    restart: always
    depends_on:
      - evolution-api
      - chatwoot-rails
      - n8n
    # PORTA ÚNICA EXPSTA: 8081
    ports:
      - "8081:80"
    volumes:
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
    networks:
      - minha_rede
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--quiet", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3

  # =============================================================================
  # ADMINER - Interface para gerenciar PostgreSQL (opcional)
  # =============================================================================
  adminer:
    image: adminer
    container_name: adminer
    restart: always
    ports:
      - "8082:8080"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - minha_rede
    environment:
      - ADMINER_DEFAULT_SERVER=postgres

# =============================================================================
# VOLUMES
# =============================================================================
volumes:
  postgres_data:
    driver: local
  lab_evolution_instances:
    driver: local
  redis_data:
    driver: local
  chatwoot_storage:
    driver: local
  n8n_data:
    driver: local

# =============================================================================
# REDES
# =============================================================================
networks:
  minha_rede:
    driver: bridge

content.content:
1→# =============================================================================
     2→# TÚNEL ÚNICO SERVEO - Nginx Reverse Proxy
     3→# =============================================================================
     4→# Script: tunnel-all.ps1
     5→# Descrição: Cria apenas 1 túnel Serveo para o Nginx (porta 8081)
     6→#           O Nginx distribui para: /evo, /chat, /n8n
     7→# Recursos: SSH Control Socket para shutdown limpo
     8→# =============================================================================
     9→
    10→# Configurações
    11→$SERVEO_SUBDOMAIN = "maceio-all-serveo"
    12→$LOCAL_PORT = 8081
    13→$SOCKET_PATH = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\tunnel-all.sock"
    14→
    15→# Cores para output
    16→function Write-ColorOutput {
    17→    param(
    18→        [Parameter(Mandatory=$true)]
    19→        [string]$Message,
    20→        [string]$Color = "White"
    21→    )
    22→    Write-Host $Message -ForegroundColor $Color
    23→}
    24→
    25→# Função: Remover socket antigo se existir
    26→function Remove-OldSocket {
    27→    if (Test-Path $SOCKET_PATH) {
    28→        Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Removendo socket antigo..." "Yellow"
    29→        Remove-Item $SOCKET_PATH -Force
    30→        Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500
    31→    }
    32→}
    33→
    34→# Função: Shutdown limpo via SSH Control Socket
    35→function Stop-TunnelViaSocket {
    36→    Write-ColorOutput "`n[CLEANUP] Iniciando shutdown limpo..." "Cyan"
    37→
    38→    if (Test-Path $SOCKET_PATH) {
    39→        Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Enviando comando 'exit' via Control Socket..." "Yellow"
    40→
    41→        # Enviar comando de shutdown ao servidor Serveo
    42→        $sshExit = ssh -S $SOCKET_PATH -O exit serveo.net 2>&1
    43→
    44→        if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
    45→            Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Shutdown limpo enviado com sucesso!" "Green"
    46→        } else {
    47→            Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Aviso: Código de saída: $LASTEXITCODE" "Yellow"
    48→        }
    49→
    50→        # Remover socket local
    51→        Remove-Item $SOCKET_PATH -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    52→    } else {
    53→        Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Socket não encontrado, limpando processo SSH..." "Yellow"
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    Write-ColorOutput "[CLEANUP] Cleanup finalizado" "Green"
    57→}
    58→
    59→# Função: Cleanup ao sair (chamado pelo trap)
    60→function Cleanup-OnExit {
    61→    Stop-TunnelViaSocket
    62→
    63→    # Matar processo SSH se ainda estiver rodando
    64→    $sshProcess = Get-Process ssh -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object {
    65→        $_.CommandLine -like "*$LOCAL_PORT*" -or $_.CommandLine -like "*serveo.net*"
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    if ($sshProcess) {
    69→        Write-ColorOutput "[CLEANUP] Finalizando processo SSH..." "Yellow"
    70→        $sshProcess | Stop-Process -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    71→    }
    72→
    73→    Write-ColorOutput "`n[✓] Túnel encerrado corretamente" "Green"
    74→}
    75→
    76→# Função: Verificar se Nginx está rodando
    77→function Test-Nginx {
    78→    Write-ColorOutput "[NGINX] Verificando se Nginx está rodando na porta $LOCAL_PORT..." "Cyan"
    79→
    80→    try {
    81→        $response = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "http://localhost:$LOCAL_PORT/health" -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 5
    82→        if ($response.StatusCode -eq 200) {
    83→            Write-ColorOutput "[NGINX] ✓ Nginx respondendo corretamente!" "Green"
    84→            return $true
    85→        }
    86→    } catch {
    87→        Write-ColorOutput "[ERRO] Nginx não está respondendo na porta $LOCAL_PORT" "Red"
    88→        Write-ColorOutput "[DICA] Execute: docker compose up -d" "Yellow"
    89→        return $false
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    return $false
    93→}
    94→
    95→# =============================================================================
    96→# INÍCIO DO SCRIPT
    97→# =============================================================================
    98→
    99→Clear-Host
   100→Write-ColorOutput "
   101→╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
   102→║         🚀 TÚNEL ÚNICO SERVEO - Nginx Reverse Proxy            ║
   103→╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
   104→║  Serviço: Nginx (porta $LOCAL_PORT)                              ║
   105→║  URL: https://$SERVEO_SUBDOMAIN.serveousercontent.com          ║
   106→║                                                                  ║
   107→║  Rotas disponíveis:                                              ║
   108→║    • /n8n  → N8N (Automação)                                     ║
   109→║    • /evo  → Evolution API (WhatsApp)                           ║
   110→║    • /chat → Chatwoot (Atendimento)                              ║
   111→╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
   112→" "Cyan"
   113→
   114→# 1. Verificar se Nginx está rodando
   115→if (-not (Test-Nginx)) {
   116→    Write-ColorOutput "`n[ERRO] Por favor, inicie o Docker primeiro:" "Red"
   117→    Write-ColorOutput "  docker compose up -d`n" "Yellow"
   118→    exit 1
   119→}
   120→
   121→# 2. Limpar socket antigo se existir
   122→Remove-OldSocket
   123→
   124→# 3. Configurar trap handler para Ctrl+C
   125→$trapScript = {
   126→    Cleanup-OnExit
   127→    exit 0
   128→}
   129→Set-PSDebug -Off
   130→[Console]::TreatControlCAsInput = $false
   131→
   132→# Registrar o handler de cleanup
   133→$null = Register-EngineEvent -SourceIdentifier PowerShell.Exiting -Action {
   134→    Cleanup-OnExit
   135→} -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   136→
   137→# 4. Criar diretório para socket se não existir
   138→$socketDir = Split-Path $SOCKET_PATH
   139→if (-not (Test-Path $socketDir)) {
   140→    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $socketDir -Force | Out-Null
   141→}
   142→
   143→# 5. Iniciar túnel com SSH Control Socket
   144→Write-ColorOutput "[TÚNEL] Iniciando túnel Serveo..." "Cyan"
   145→Write-ColorOutput "[INFO] Subdomínio: $SERVEO_SUBDOMAIN" "White"
   146→Write-ColorOutput "[INFO] Porta local: $LOCAL_PORT" "White"
   147→Write-ColorOutput "[INFO] Socket: $SOCKET_PATH" "White"
   148→Write-ColorOutput "`n[⏳] Conectando ao Serveo.net..." "Yellow"
   149→
   150→# Comando SSH com Control Socket
   151→$sshCommand = @"
   152→ssh -M -S "$SOCKET_PATH" -o ServerAliveInterval=60 -o ExitOnForwardFailure=yes -N -R ${SERVEO_SUBDOMAIN}:80:localhost:${LOCAL_PORT} serveo.net
   153→"@
   154→
   155→# Executar SSH em background
   156→$sshProcess = Start-Process -FilePath "ssh" -ArgumentList `
   157→    "-M", `
   158→    "-S", "$SOCKET_PATH", `
   159→    "-o", "ServerAliveInterval=60", `
   160→    "-o", "ExitOnForwardFailure=yes", `
   161→    "-N", `
   162→    "-R", "${SERVEO_SUBDOMAIN}:80:localhost:${LOCAL_PORT}", `
   163→    "serveo.net" `
   164→    -PassThru -WindowStyle Hidden
   165→
   166→# Aguardar alguns segundos para o túnel estabelecer
   167→Start-Sleep -Seconds 5
   168→
   169→# 6. Verificar se o túnel foi criado com sucesso
   170→Write-ColorOutput "`n[CHECK] Verificando status do túnel..." "Cyan"
   171→
   172→if (Test-Path $SOCKET_PATH) {
   173→    Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] ✓ Control Socket criado: $SOCKET_PATH" "Green"
   174→} else {
   175→    Write-ColorOutput "[AVISO] Socket não encontrado, mas continuando..." "Yellow"
   176→}
   177→
   178→# Verificar se processo SSH está rodando
   179→$sshCheck = Get-Process ssh -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object {
   180→    $_.Id -eq $sshProcess.Id
   181→}
   182→
   183→if ($sshCheck) {
   184→    Write-ColorOutput "[SSH] ✓ Processo SSH ativo (PID: $($sshProcess.Id))" "Green"
   185→
   186→    Write-ColorOutput "`n╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗" "Green"
   187→    Write-ColorOutput "║  ✓ TÚNEL ATIVO E FUNCIONANDO!                            ║" "Green"
   188→    Write-ColorOutput "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝" "Green"
   189→    Write-ColorOutput "`n🌐 URLs Públicas:" "Cyan"
   190→    Write-ColorOutput "   • N8N:        https://$SERVEO_SUBDOMAIN.serveousercontent.com/n8n/" "White"
   191→    Write-ColorOutput "   • Evolution:  https://$SERVEO_SUBDOMAIN.serveousercontent.com/evo/" "White"
   192→    Write-ColorOutput "   • Chatwoot:   https://$SERVEO_SUBDOMAIN.serveousercontent.com/chat/" "White"
   193→    Write-ColorOutput "`n⚠️  Pressione Ctrl+C para encerrar o túnel corretamente" "Yellow"
   194→    Write-ColorOutput "`n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━`n" "Gray"
   195→
   196→    # Manter script rodando e monitorar processo SSH
   197→    try {
   198→        while ($sshProcess -and !$sshProcess.HasExited) {
   199→            Start-Sleep -Seconds 5
   200→
   201→            # Verificar se socket ainda existe
   202→            if (-not (Test-Path $SOCKET_PATH)) {
   203→                Write-ColorOutput "[⚠️] Socket perdido, verificando processo..." "Yellow"
   204→            }
   205→
   206→            # Verificar se processo ainda está rodando
   207→            $sshProcess.Refresh()
   208→            if ($sshProcess.HasExited) {
   209→                Write-ColorOutput "[❌] Processo SSH encerrado inesperadamente!" "Red"
   210→                break
   211→            }
   212→        }
   213→    } finally {
   214→        # Cleanup ao sair
   215→        Cleanup-OnExit
   216→    }
   217→
   218→} else {
   219→    Write-ColorOutput "[ERRO] Processo SSH não iniciou corretamente!" "Red"
   220→    Write-ColorOutput "[DICA] Verifique sua conexão com a internet" "Yellow"
   221→    exit 1
   222→}
   223→

1→# ============================================================================
     2→# TUNNEL SERVEO - EVOLUTION API (localhost:8080)
     3→# Subdomain: maceio-evo-v1.serveousercontent.com
     4→# ============================================================================
     5→# MELHORIA: SSH Control Socket para desligamento limpo
     6→# ============================================================================
     7→
     8→param(
     9→    [switch]$NoReconnect
    10→)
    11→
    12→# Configuration
    13→$SUBDOMAIN = "maceio-evo-v1"
    14→$LOCAL_PORT = "8080"
    15→$SERVEO_HOST = "serveo.net"
    16→$SERVICE_NAME = "Evolution API"
    17→$SOCKET_PATH = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\tunnel-evolution.sock"
    18→
    19→# Output function
    20→function Write-ColorOutput {
    21→    param(
    22→        [string]$Message,
    23→        [string]$Color = "White"
    24→    )
    25→    Write-Host "[$(Get-Date -Format 'HH:mm:ss')] $Message" -ForegroundColor $Color
    26→}
    27→
    28→# ============================================================================
    29→# FUNCTION: Kill tunnel via SSH control socket (clean shutdown)
    30→# ============================================================================
    31→function Stop-TunnelViaSocket {
    32→    Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Attempting clean shutdown via control socket..." "Cyan"
    33→
    34→    try {
    35→        # Check if socket exists and is active
    36→        $checkResult = & ssh -S "$SOCKET_PATH" -O check "$SERVEO_HOST" 2>&1
    37→
    38→        if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
    39→            # Socket is active - exit cleanly
    40→            $null = & ssh -S "$SOCKET_PATH" -O exit "$SERVEO_HOST" 2>&1
    41→            Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Clean shutdown initiated" "Green"
    42→            Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500
    43→            return $true
    44→        } else {
    45→            Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] No active control socket found" "Yellow"
    46→            return $false
    47→        }
    48→    } catch {
    49→        Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Control socket command failed: $_" "Yellow"
    50→        return $false
    51→    }
    52→}
    53→
    54→# ============================================================================
    55→# FUNCTION: Remove old socket file if exists
    56→# ============================================================================
    57→function Remove-OldSocket {
    58→    if (Test-Path "$SOCKET_PATH") {
    59→        Write-ColorOutput "[CLEAN] Removing old socket file: $SOCKET_PATH" "Yellow"
    60→        try {
    61→            Remove-Item "$SOCKET_PATH" -Force -ErrorAction Stop
    62→            Write-ColorOutput "[OK] Old socket removed" "Green"
    63→        } catch {
    64→            Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Could not remove socket: $_" "Yellow"
    65→        }
    66→    }
    67→}
    68→
    69→# ============================================================================
    70→# FUNCTION: Remove ALL Serveo tunnels (aggressive cleanup)
    71→# ============================================================================
    72→function Remove-OrphanTunnels {
    73→    Write-ColorOutput "[CLEAN] Looking for ALL Serveo SSH processes..." "Cyan"
    74→
    75→    try {
    76→        # Find ALL SSH processes connecting to serveo.net
    77→        $sshProcesses = Get-CimInstance Win32_Process | Where-Object {
    78→            $_.Name -eq 'ssh.exe' -and
    79→            $_.CommandLine -match 'serveo\.net'
    80→        } | Sort-Object ProcessId -Descending
    81→
    82→        if ($sshProcesses.Count -eq 0) {
    83→            Write-ColorOutput "[OK] No Serveo tunnels found" "Green"
    84→            return $true
    85→        }
    86→
    87→        Write-ColorOutput "[CLEAN] Found $($sshProcesses.Count) Serveo tunnel(s) - REMOVING ALL!" "Yellow"
    88→
    89→        # KILL ALL Serveo processes
    90→        foreach ($proc in $sshProcesses) {

    92→                Stop-Process -Id $proc.ProcessId -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    93→                Write-ColorOutput "  [KILLED] PID $($proc.ProcessId)" "Red"
    94→                Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 100
    95→            } catch {
    96→                Write-ColorOutput "  [ERROR] Failed to kill PID $($proc.ProcessId): $_" "Red"
    97→            }
    98→        }
    99→
   100→        Write-ColorOutput "[OK] All Serveo tunnels removed" "Green"

   106→    }

   138→# ============================================================================
   139→# FUNCTION: Start SSH tunnel with CONTROL SOCKET
   140→# ============================================================================
   141→function Start-ServeoTunnel {
   142→    Write-ColorOutput "[START] Starting Serveo tunnel for $SERVICE_NAME..." "Cyan"
   143→    Write-ColorOutput "   Subdomain: $SUBDOMAIN.$SERVEO_HOST" "Cyan"
   144→    Write-ColorOutput "   Local: localhost:$LOCAL_PORT" "Cyan"
   145→    Write-ColorOutput "   Control Socket: $SOCKET_PATH" "Cyan"
   146→    Write-ColorOutput ""
   147→
   148→    # Ensure .ssh directory exists
   149→    $sshDir = Split-Path "$SOCKET_PATH" -Parent
   150→    if (-not (Test-Path "$sshDir")) {
   151→        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$sshDir" -Force | Out-Null
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    # Remove old socket if exists
   155→    Remove-OldSocket
   156→
   157→    # SSH options:
   158→    # -M: Master mode (allows control socket)
   159→    # -S <path>: Control socket path
   160→    # -f: Daemonize (run in background)
   161→    # -N: No remote command (just tunneling)
   162→    # -R: Remote port forwarding
   163→    # - ServerAliveInterval=60: Send keep-alive every 60 seconds
   164→    # - ServerAliveCountMax=3: After 3 failures, terminate connection
   165→    # - ExitOnForwardFailure=yes: Terminate if tunnel creation fails
   166→    # - StrictHostKeyChecking=no: Don't ask for host key confirmation
   167→    $sshArgs = @(
   168→        "-M", "-S", "$SOCKET_PATH",
   169→        "-f", "-N",
   170→        "-R", "${SUBDOMAIN}:80:localhost:${LOCAL_PORT}",
   171→        "-o", "ServerAliveInterval=60",
   172→        "-o", "ServerAliveCountMax=3",
   173→        "-o", "ExitOnForwardFailure=yes",
   174→        "-o", "StrictHostKeyChecking=no",
   175→        "-o", "UserKnownHostsFile=nul",
   176→        $SERVEO_HOST
   177→    )
   178→
   179→    Write-ColorOutput "[CONNECT] Connecting to Serveo with control socket..." "Yellow"
   180→
   181→    try {
   182→        # Start SSH process with control socket
   183→        $process = Start-Process -FilePath "ssh" -ArgumentList $sshArgs -PassThru -NoNewWindow
   184→
   185→        # Wait for connection to establish
   186→        Write-ColorOutput "[WAIT] Waiting for tunnel to be ready..." "Yellow"
   187→        Start-Sleep -Seconds 10
   188→
   189→        # Check if process is still running
   190→        $stillRunning = Get-Process -Id $process.Id -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   191→
   192→        if (-not $stillRunning) {
   193→            Write-ColorOutput "[ERROR] SSH process terminated unexpectedly" "Red"
   194→            Remove-OldSocket
   195→            return $null
   196→        }
   197→
   198→        # Verify control socket is working
   199→        Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Verifying control socket..." "Yellow"
   200→        $socketCheck = & ssh -S "$SOCKET_PATH" -O check "$SERVEO_HOST" 2>&1
   201→
   202→        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
   203→            Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Control socket not responding (this is OK on some systems)" "Yellow"
   204→        } else {
   205→            Write-ColorOutput "[OK] Control socket active" "Green"
   206→        }
   207→
   208→        # Verify tunnel is actually working by testing the public URL
   209→        Write-ColorOutput "[VERIFY] Testing if tunnel is working..." "Yellow"
   210→        $publicUrl = "https://${SUBDOMAIN}.serveousercontent.com"
   211→        $working = $false
   212→
   213→        # Try up to 3 times to verify
   214→        for ($i = 1; $i -le 3; $i++) {
   215→            Write-ColorOutput "[VERIFY] Attempt $i of 3..." "Cyan"
   216→
   217→            if (Test-PublicURL -Url $publicUrl -Timeout 5) {
   218→                $working = $true
   219→                break
   220→            }
   221→
   222→            if ($i -lt 3) {
   223→                Write-ColorOutput "[VERIFY] Not working yet, waiting 3 more seconds..." "Yellow"
   224→                Start-Sleep -Seconds 3
   225→            }
   226→        }
   227→
   228→        if ($working) {
   229→            Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   230→            Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Green"
   231→            Write-ColorOutput "=  TUNNEL ACTIVE AND WORKING!                             =" "Green"
   232→            Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Green"
   233→            Write-ColorOutput "=  URL: https://$SUBDOMAIN.serveousercontent.com         =" "White"
   234→            Write-ColorOutput "=  PID: $($process.Id)                                      =" "White"
   235→            Write-ColorOutput "=  STATUS: VERIFIED (URL tested)                          =" "Green"
   236→            Write-ColorOutput "=  SOCKET: Control socket enabled                         =" "Green"
   237→            Write-ColorOutput "=                                                         =" "White"
   238→            Write-ColorOutput "=  Press Ctrl+C to stop this tunnel                       =" "Yellow"
   239→            Write-ColorOutput "=  (Clean shutdown via control socket)                    =" "Yellow"
   240→            Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Green"
   241→            Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   242→
   243→            return $process
   244→        } else {
   245→            Write-ColorOutput "[ERROR] Tunnel created but URL not responding!" "Red"
   246→            Write-ColorOutput "[ERROR] Orphan tunnel exists on Serveo - trying aggressive cleanup..." "Red"
   247→
   248→            # Clean shutdown via socket
   249→            Stop-TunnelViaSocket
   250→
   251→            # Kill the process
   252→            try {
   253→                Stop-Process -Id $process.Id -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   254→            } catch {}
   255→
   256→            Remove-OldSocket
   257→            return $null
   258→        }
   259→    } catch {
   260→        Write-ColorOutput "[ERROR] Error starting tunnel: $_" "Red"
   261→        Remove-OldSocket
   262→        return $null
   263→    }
   264→}

   266→# ============================================================================
   267→# CLEANUP FUNCTION: Called on script exit (Ctrl+C, etc.)
   268→# ============================================================================
   269→function Cleanup-OnExit {
   270→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   271→    Write-ColorOutput "[CLEANUP] Cleaning up tunnel..." "Yellow"
   272→
   273→    # Try clean shutdown via socket first
   274→    Stop-TunnelViaSocket
   275→
   276→    # Remove socket file
   277→    Remove-OldSocket
   278→
   279→    Write-ColorOutput "[CLEANUP] Cleanup complete" "Green"
   280→}
   281→
   282→# ============================================================================
   283→# MAIN SCRIPT
   284→# ============================================================================
   285→
   286→# Set up cleanup handler for Ctrl+C
   287→$null = Register-EngineEvent -SourceIdentifier PowerShell.Exiting -Action {
   288→    Cleanup-OnExit
   289→}
   290→
   291→# Trap Ctrl+C
   292→trap {
   293→    Cleanup-OnExit
   294→    exit 0
   295→}
   296→
   297→Clear-Host
   298→
   299→# No delay - start immediately
   300→Write-ColorOutput "[STARTUP] Connecting immediately..." "Cyan"
   301→Write-ColorOutput ""
   302→Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Cyan"
   303→Write-ColorOutput "=     TUNNEL SERVEO - EVOLUTION API                        =" "Cyan"
   304→Write-ColorOutput "=     localhost:8080 -> maceio-evo-v1.serveousercontent.com =" "Cyan"
   305→Write-ColorOutput "=     WITH SSH CONTROL SOCKET                             =" "Cyan"
   306→Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Cyan"
   307→Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   308→
   309→# Check if local service is running
   310→Write-ColorOutput "[CHECK] Verifying if $SERVICE_NAME is running on port $LOCAL_PORT..." "Yellow"
   311→if (-not (Test-LocalService)) {
   312→    Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] $SERVICE_NAME doesn't seem to be responding!" "Yellow"
   313→    Write-ColorOutput "   Check if Docker is running: docker compose ps" "Yellow"
   314→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   315→    $response = Read-Host "Continue anyway? (s/N)"
   316→    if ($response -ne "s" -and $response -ne "S") {
   317→        Write-ColorOutput "[ABORT] Operation cancelled" "Red"
   318→        exit 1
   319→    }
   320→}
   321→
   322→# Clean orphan tunnels before starting
   323→$cleanResult = Remove-OrphanTunnels
   324→if (-not $cleanResult) {
   325→    Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Continuing anyway..." "Yellow"
   326→}
   327→
   328→Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   329→
   330→# Main auto-reconnect loop
   331→do {
   332→    $tunnelProcess = Start-ServeoTunnel
   333→
   334→    if ($null -eq $tunnelProcess) {
   335→        Write-ColorOutput "[RETRY] Server blocking subdomain - reconnecting aggressively..." "Yellow"
   336→        # NO delay - retry immediately to force Serveo release
   337→        continue
   338→    }
   339→
   340→    # Wait for process to finish (or Ctrl+C)
   341→    try {
   342→        $tunnelProcess.WaitForExit()
   343→    } catch [System.Management.Automation.PipelineStoppedException] {
   344→        # Ctrl+C pressed - trap will handle cleanup
   345→        Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   346→        Write-ColorOutput "[STOP] Interrupt detected!" "Yellow"
   347→        Cleanup-OnExit
   348→        break
   349→    }
   350→
   351→    # If we got here, process terminated unexpectedly
   352→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   353→    Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Connection terminated unexpectedly!" "Yellow"
   354→    Write-ColorOutput "   Reconnecting immediately..." "Yellow"
   355→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   356→
   357→    # Cleanup before reconnecting
   358→    Cleanup-OnExit
   359→
   360→    # NO DELAY - reconnect immediately
   361→    # Check orphan tunnels again before reconnecting
   362→    Remove-OrphanTunnels
   363→
   364→} while (-not $NoReconnect)
   365→
   366→Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   367→Write-ColorOutput "[DONE] Script finished" "Cyan"
   368→

    28→# ============================================================================
    29→# FUNCTION: Kill tunnel via SSH control socket (clean shutdown)
    30→# ============================================================================
    31→function Stop-TunnelViaSocket {
    32→    Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Attempting clean shutdown via control socket..." "Cyan"
    33→
    34→    try {
    35→        # Check if socket exists and is active
    36→        $checkResult = & ssh -S "$SOCKET_PATH" -O check "$SERVEO_HOST" 2>&1
    37→

    41→            Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Clean shutdown initiated" "Green"
    42→            Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500
    43→            return $true
    44→        } else {
    45→            Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] No active control socket found" "Yellow"
    46→            return $false
    47→        }

    54→# ============================================================================
    55→# FUNCTION: Remove old socket file if exists
    56→# ============================================================================
    57→function Remove-OldSocket {
    58→    if (Test-Path "$SOCKET_PATH") {

    60→        try {
    61→            Remove-Item "$SOCKET_PATH" -Force -ErrorAction Stop
    62→            Write-ColorOutput "[OK] Old socket removed" "Green"

    69→# ============================================================================
    70→# FUNCTION: Remove ALL Serveo tunnels (aggressive cleanup)

    72→function Remove-OrphanTunnels {

    81→
    82→        if ($sshProcesses.Count -eq 0) {

    85→        }
    86→
    87→        Write-ColorOutput "[CLEAN] Found $($sshProcesses.Count) Serveo tunnel(s) - REMOVING ALL!" "Yellow"
    88→
    89→        # KILL ALL Serveo processes
    90→        foreach ($proc in $sshProcesses) {
    91→            try {
    92→                Stop-Process -Id $proc.ProcessId -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    93→                Write-ColorOutput "  [KILLED] PID $($proc.ProcessId)" "Red"
    94→                Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 100
    95→            } catch {
    96→                Write-ColorOutput "  [ERROR] Failed to kill PID $($proc.ProcessId): $_" "Red"
    97→            }
    98→        }
    99→
   100→        Write-ColorOutput "[OK] All Serveo tunnels removed" "Green"
   101→        Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
   102→        return $true
   103→    } catch {

   106→    }
   107→}

   310→Write-ColorOutput "[CHECK] Verifying if $SERVICE_NAME is running on port $LOCAL_PORT..." "Yellow"
   311→if (-not (Test-LocalService)) {
   312→    Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] $SERVICE_NAME doesn't seem to be responding!" "Yellow"
   313→    Write-ColorOutput "   Check if Docker is running: docker compose ps" "Yellow"
   314→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   315→    $response = Read-Host "Continue anyway? (s/N)"
   316→    if ($response -ne "s" -and $response -ne "S") {
   317→        Write-ColorOutput "[ABORT] Operation cancelled" "Red"
   318→        exit 1
   319→    }
   320→}
   321→
   322→# Clean orphan tunnels before starting
   323→$cleanResult = Remove-OrphanTunnels
   324→if (-not $cleanResult) {
   325→    Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Continuing anyway..." "Yellow"
   326→}
   327→
   328→Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   329→
   330→# Main auto-reconnect loop
   331→do {
   332→    $tunnelProcess = Start-ServeoTunnel
   333→
   334→    if ($null -eq $tunnelProcess) {
   335→        Write-ColorOutput "[RETRY] Server blocking subdomain - reconnecting aggressively..." "Yellow"
   336→        # NO delay - retry immediately to force Serveo release
   337→        continue
   338→    }
   339→
   340→    # Wait for process to finish (or Ctrl+C)
   341→    try {
   342→        $tunnelProcess.WaitForExit()
   343→    } catch [System.Management.Automation.PipelineStoppedException] {
   344→        # Ctrl+C pressed - trap will handle cleanup
   345→        Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   346→        Write-ColorOutput "[STOP] Interrupt detected!" "Yellow"
   347→        Cleanup-OnExit
   348→        break
   349→    }
   350→
   351→    # If we got here, process terminated unexpectedly
   352→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   353→    Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Connection terminated unexpectedly!" "Yellow"
   354→    Write-ColorOutput "   Reconnecting immediately..." "Yellow"
   355→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   356→
   357→    # Cleanup before reconnecting
   358→    Cleanup-OnExit
   359→
   360→    # NO DELAY - reconnect immediately
   361→    # Check orphan tunnels again before reconnecting
   362→    Remove-OrphanTunnels
   363→
   364→} while (-not $NoReconnect)
   365→
   366→Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   367→Write-ColorOutput "[DONE] Script finished" "Cyan"
   368→

1→# ============================================================================
     2→# TUNNEL SERVEO - N8N (localhost:5678)
     3→# Subdomain: maceio-n8n-alfa42.serveousercontent.com
     4→# ============================================================================
     5→# MELHORIA: SSH Control Socket para desligamento limpo
     6→# ============================================================================
     7→
     8→param(
     9→    [switch]$NoReconnect
    10→)
    11→
    12→# Configuration
    13→$SUBDOMAIN = "maceio-n8n-alfa42"
    14→$LOCAL_PORT = "5678"
    15→$SERVEO_HOST = "serveo.net"
    16→$SERVICE_NAME = "N8N"
    17→$SOCKET_PATH = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\tunnel-n8n.sock"
    18→
    19→# Output function
    20→function Write-ColorOutput {
    21→    param(
    22→        [string]$Message,
    23→        [string]$Color = "White"
    24→    )
    25→    Write-Host "[$(Get-Date -Format 'HH:mm:ss')] $Message" -ForegroundColor $Color
    26→}
    27→
    28→# ============================================================================
    29→# FUNCTION: Kill tunnel via SSH control socket (clean shutdown)
    30→# ============================================================================
    31→function Stop-TunnelViaSocket {
    32→    Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Attempting clean shutdown via control socket..." "Cyan"
    33→
    34→    try {
    35→        # Check if socket exists and is active
    36→        $checkResult = & ssh -S "$SOCKET_PATH" -O check "$SERVEO_HOST" 2>&1
    37→
    38→        if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
    39→            # Socket is active - exit cleanly
    40→            $null = & ssh -S "$SOCKET_PATH" -O exit "$SERVEO_HOST" 2>&1
    41→            Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Clean shutdown initiated" "Green"
    42→            Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500
    43→            return $true
    44→        } else {
    45→            Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] No active control socket found" "Yellow"
    46→            return $false
    47→        }
    48→    } catch {
    49→        Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Control socket command failed: $_" "Yellow"
    50→        return $false
    51→    }
    52→}
    53→
    54→# ============================================================================
    55→# FUNCTION: Remove old socket file if exists
    56→# ============================================================================
    57→function Remove-OldSocket {
    58→    if (Test-Path "$SOCKET_PATH") {
    59→        Write-ColorOutput "[CLEAN] Removing old socket file: $SOCKET_PATH" "Yellow"
    60→        try {
    61→            Remove-Item "$SOCKET_PATH" -Force -ErrorAction Stop
    62→            Write-ColorOutput "[OK] Old socket removed" "Green"
    63→        } catch {
    64→            Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Could not remove socket: $_" "Yellow"
    65→        }
    66→    }
    67→}
    68→
    69→# ============================================================================
    70→# FUNCTION: Remove ALL Serveo tunnels (aggressive cleanup)
    71→# ============================================================================
    72→function Remove-OrphanTunnels {
    73→    Write-ColorOutput "[CLEAN] Looking for ALL Serveo SSH processes..." "Cyan"
    74→
    75→    try {
    76→        # Find ALL SSH processes connecting to serveo.net
    77→        $sshProcesses = Get-CimInstance Win32_Process | Where-Object {
    78→            $_.Name -eq 'ssh.exe' -and
    79→            $_.CommandLine -match 'serveo\.net'
    80→        } | Sort-Object ProcessId -Descending
    81→
    82→        if ($sshProcesses.Count -eq 0) {
    83→            Write-ColorOutput "[OK] No Serveo tunnels found" "Green"
    84→            return $true
    85→        }
    86→
    87→        Write-ColorOutput "[CLEAN] Found $($sshProcesses.Count) Serveo tunnel(s) - REMOVING ALL!" "Yellow"
    88→
    89→        # KILL ALL Serveo processes
    90→        foreach ($proc in $sshProcesses) {
    91→            try {
    92→                Stop-Process -Id $proc.ProcessId -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    93→                Write-ColorOutput "  [KILLED] PID $($proc.ProcessId)" "Red"
    94→                Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 100
    95→            } catch {
    96→                Write-ColorOutput "  [ERROR] Failed to kill PID $($proc.ProcessId): $_" "Red"
    97→            }
    98→        }
    99→
   100→        Write-ColorOutput "[OK] All Serveo tunnels removed" "Green"
   101→        Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
   102→        return $true
   103→    } catch {
   104→        Write-ColorOutput "[ERROR] Error removing tunnels: $_" "Red"
   105→        return $false
   106→    }
   107→}
   108→
   109→# ============================================================================
   110→# FUNCTION: Test if local service is responding
   111→# ============================================================================
   112→function Test-LocalService {
   113→    try {
   114→        $response = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "http://localhost:$LOCAL_PORT" -Method Head -TimeoutSec 3 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   115→        return $response.StatusCode -eq 200
   116→    } catch {
   117→        return $false
   118→    }
   119→}
   120→
   121→# ============================================================================
   122→# FUNCTION: Test if public URL is responding
   123→# ============================================================================
   124→function Test-PublicURL {
   125→    param(
   126→        [string]$Url,
   127→        [int]$Timeout = 10
   128→    )
   129→
   130→    try {
   131→        $response = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $Url -Method Head -TimeoutSec $Timeout -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   132→        return $true
   133→    } catch {
   134→        return $false
   135→    }
   136→}
   137→
   138→# ============================================================================
   139→# FUNCTION: Start SSH tunnel with CONTROL SOCKET
   140→# ============================================================================
   141→function Start-ServeoTunnel {
   142→    Write-ColorOutput "[START] Starting Serveo tunnel for $SERVICE_NAME..." "Cyan"
   143→    Write-ColorOutput "   Subdomain: $SUBDOMAIN.$SERVEO_HOST" "Cyan"
   144→    Write-ColorOutput "   Local: localhost:$LOCAL_PORT" "Cyan"
   145→    Write-ColorOutput "   Control Socket: $SOCKET_PATH" "Cyan"
   146→    Write-ColorOutput ""
   147→
   148→    # Ensure .ssh directory exists
   149→    $sshDir = Split-Path "$SOCKET_PATH" -Parent
   150→    if (-not (Test-Path "$sshDir")) {
   151→        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$sshDir" -Force | Out-Null
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    # Remove old socket if exists
   155→    Remove-OldSocket
   156→
   157→    # SSH options:
   158→    # -M: Master mode (allows control socket)
   159→    # -S <path>: Control socket path
   160→    # -f: Daemonize (run in background)
   161→    # -N: No remote command (just tunneling)
   162→    # -R: Remote port forwarding
   163→    # - ServerAliveInterval=60: Send keep-alive every 60 seconds
   164→    # - ServerAliveCountMax=3: After 3 failures, terminate connection
   165→    # - ExitOnForwardFailure=yes: Terminate if tunnel creation fails
   166→    # - StrictHostKeyChecking=no: Don't ask for host key confirmation
   167→    $sshArgs = @(
   168→        "-M", "-S", "$SOCKET_PATH",
   169→        "-f", "-N",
   170→        "-R", "${SUBDOMAIN}:80:localhost:${LOCAL_PORT}",
   171→        "-o", "ServerAliveInterval=60",
   172→        "-o", "ServerAliveCountMax=3",
   173→        "-o", "ExitOnForwardFailure=yes",
   174→        "-o", "StrictHostKeyChecking=no",
   175→        "-o", "UserKnownHostsFile=nul",
   176→        $SERVEO_HOST
   177→    )
   178→
   179→    Write-ColorOutput "[CONNECT] Connecting to Serveo with control socket..." "Yellow"
   180→
   181→    try {
   182→        # Start SSH process with control socket
   183→        $process = Start-Process -FilePath "ssh" -ArgumentList $sshArgs -PassThru -NoNewWindow
   184→
   185→        # Wait for connection to establish
   186→        Write-ColorOutput "[WAIT] Waiting for tunnel to be ready..." "Yellow"
   187→        Start-Sleep -Seconds 10
   188→
   189→        # Check if process is still running
   190→        $stillRunning = Get-Process -Id $process.Id -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   191→
   192→        if (-not $stillRunning) {
   193→            Write-ColorOutput "[ERROR] SSH process terminated unexpectedly" "Red"
   194→            Remove-OldSocket
   195→            return $null
   196→        }
   197→
   198→        # Verify control socket is working
   199→        Write-ColorOutput "[SOCKET] Verifying control socket..." "Yellow"
   200→        $socketCheck = & ssh -S "$SOCKET_PATH" -O check "$SERVEO_HOST" 2>&1
   201→
   202→        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
   203→            Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Control socket not responding (this is OK on some systems)" "Yellow"
   204→        } else {
   205→            Write-ColorOutput "[OK] Control socket active" "Green"
   206→        }
   207→
   208→        # Verify tunnel is actually working by testing the public URL
   209→        Write-ColorOutput "[VERIFY] Testing if tunnel is working..." "Yellow"
   210→        $publicUrl = "https://${SUBDOMAIN}.serveousercontent.com"
   211→        $working = $false
   212→
   213→        # Try up to 3 times to verify
   214→        for ($i = 1; $i -le 3; $i++) {
   215→            Write-ColorOutput "[VERIFY] Attempt $i of 3..." "Cyan"
   216→
   217→            if (Test-PublicURL -Url $publicUrl -Timeout 5) {
   218→                $working = $true
   219→                break
   220→            }
   221→
   222→            if ($i -lt 3) {
   223→                Write-ColorOutput "[VERIFY] Not working yet, waiting 3 more seconds..." "Yellow"
   224→                Start-Sleep -Seconds 3
   225→            }
   226→        }
   227→
   228→        if ($working) {
   229→            Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   230→            Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Green"
   231→            Write-ColorOutput "=  TUNNEL ACTIVE AND WORKING!                             =" "Green"
   232→            Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Green"
   233→            Write-ColorOutput "=  URL: https://$SUBDOMAIN.serveousercontent.com         =" "White"
   234→            Write-ColorOutput "=  PID: $($process.Id)                                      =" "White"
   235→            Write-ColorOutput "=  STATUS: VERIFIED (URL tested)                          =" "Green"
   236→            Write-ColorOutput "=  SOCKET: Control socket enabled                         =" "Green"
   237→            Write-ColorOutput "=                                                         =" "White"
   238→            Write-ColorOutput "=  Press Ctrl+C to stop this tunnel                       =" "Yellow"
   239→            Write-ColorOutput "=  (Clean shutdown via control socket)                    =" "Yellow"
   240→            Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Green"
   241→            Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   242→
   243→            return $process
   244→        } else {
   245→            Write-ColorOutput "[ERROR] Tunnel created but URL not responding!" "Red"
   246→            Write-ColorOutput "[ERROR] Orphan tunnel exists on Serveo - trying aggressive cleanup..." "Red"
   247→
   248→            # Clean shutdown via socket
   249→            Stop-TunnelViaSocket
   250→
   251→            # Kill the process
   252→            try {
   253→                Stop-Process -Id $process.Id -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   254→            } catch {}
   255→
   256→            Remove-OldSocket
   257→            return $null
   258→        }
   259→    } catch {
   260→        Write-ColorOutput "[ERROR] Error starting tunnel: $_" "Red"
   261→        Remove-OldSocket
   262→        return $null
   263→    }
   264→}
   265→
   266→# ============================================================================
   267→# CLEANUP FUNCTION: Called on script exit (Ctrl+C, etc.)
   268→# ============================================================================
   269→function Cleanup-OnExit {
   270→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   271→    Write-ColorOutput "[CLEANUP] Cleaning up tunnel..." "Yellow"
   272→
   273→    # Try clean shutdown via socket first
   274→    Stop-TunnelViaSocket
   275→
   276→    # Remove socket file
   277→    Remove-OldSocket
   278→
   279→    Write-ColorOutput "[CLEANUP] Cleanup complete" "Green"
   280→}
   281→
   282→# ============================================================================
   283→# MAIN SCRIPT
   284→# ============================================================================
   285→
   286→# Set up cleanup handler for Ctrl+C
   287→$null = Register-EngineEvent -SourceIdentifier PowerShell.Exiting -Action {
   288→    Cleanup-OnExit
   289→}
   290→
   291→# Trap Ctrl+C
   292→trap {
   293→    Cleanup-OnExit
   294→    exit 0
   295→}
   296→
   297→Clear-Host
   298→
   299→# No delay - start immediately
   300→Write-ColorOutput "[STARTUP] Connecting immediately..." "Cyan"
   301→Write-ColorOutput ""
   302→Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Cyan"
   303→Write-ColorOutput "=     TUNNEL SERVEO - N8N                                   =" "Cyan"
   304→Write-ColorOutput "=     localhost:5678 -> maceio-n8n-alfa42.serveousercontent.com =" "Cyan"
   305→Write-ColorOutput "=     WITH SSH CONTROL SOCKET                             =" "Cyan"
   306→Write-ColorOutput "============================================================" "Cyan"
   307→Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   308→
   309→# Check if local service is running
   310→Write-ColorOutput "[CHECK] Verifying if $SERVICE_NAME is running on port $LOCAL_PORT..." "Yellow"
   311→if (-not (Test-LocalService)) {
   312→    Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] $SERVICE_NAME doesn't seem to be responding!" "Yellow"
   313→    Write-ColorOutput "   Check if Docker is running: docker compose ps" "Yellow"
   314→    Write-ColorOutput "   And if N8N is started: .\start-n8n.ps1" "Yellow"
   315→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   316→    $response = Read-Host "Continue anyway? (s/N)"
   317→    if ($response -ne "s" -and $response -ne "S") {
   318→        Write-ColorOutput "[ABORT] Operation cancelled" "Red"
   319→        exit 1
   320→    }
   321→}
   322→
   323→# Clean orphan tunnels before starting
   324→$cleanResult = Remove-OrphanTunnels
   325→if (-not $cleanResult) {
   326→    Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Continuing anyway..." "Yellow"
   327→}
   328→
   329→Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   330→
   331→# Main auto-reconnect loop
   332→do {
   333→    $tunnelProcess = Start-ServeoTunnel
   334→
   335→    if ($null -eq $tunnelProcess) {
   336→        Write-ColorOutput "[RETRY] Server blocking subdomain - reconnecting aggressively..." "Yellow"
   337→        # NO delay - retry immediately to force Serveo release
   338→        continue
   339→    }
   340→
   341→    # Wait for process to finish (or Ctrl+C)
   342→    try {
   343→        $tunnelProcess.WaitForExit()
   344→    } catch [System.Management.Automation.PipelineStoppedException] {
   345→        # Ctrl+C pressed - trap will handle cleanup
   346→        Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   347→        Write-ColorOutput "[STOP] Interrupt detected!" "Yellow"
   348→        Cleanup-OnExit
   349→        break
   350→    }
   351→
   352→    # If we got here, process terminated unexpectedly
   353→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   354→    Write-ColorOutput "[WARN] Connection terminated unexpectedly!" "Yellow"
   355→    Write-ColorOutput "   Reconnecting immediately..." "Yellow"
   356→    Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   357→
   358→    # Cleanup before reconnecting
   359→    Cleanup-OnExit
   360→
   361→    # NO DELAY - reconnect immediately
   362→    # Check orphan tunnels again before reconnecting
   363→    Remove-OrphanTunnels
   364→
   365→} while (-not $NoReconnect)
   366→
   367→Write-ColorOutput "" "White"
   368→Write-ColorOutput "[DONE] Script finished" "Cyan"
   369→

# =============================================================================
# SERVEO N8N - SCRIPT COM VARIÁVEIS DE AMBIENTE CONFIGURADAS
# =============================================================================
# Este script inicia o N8N localmente com as variáveis de ambiente
# corretas para integração com Serveo
# =============================================================================

Write-Output "="*80
Write-Output "  INICIALIZANDO N8N - Integração com Serveo"
Write-Output "="*80
Write-Output ""

# Configurar variáveis de ambiente
Write-Output "Configurando variáveis de ambiente..."
$env:N8N_ALLOW_EXECUTE_COMMAND = "true"
$env:NODES_EXCLUDE = ""
$env:WEBHOOK_URL = "https://maceio-n8n-alfa42.serveousercontent.com"
$env:N8N_HOST = "localhost"
$env:N8N_PORT = "5678"
$env:N8N_PROTOCOL = "https"

Write-Output "✓ N8N_ALLOW_EXECUTE_COMMAND=$env:N8N_ALLOW_EXECUTE_COMMAND"
Write-Output "✓ NODES_EXCLUDE=$env:NODES_EXCLUDE"
Write-Output "✓ WEBHOOK_URL=$env:WEBHOOK_URL"
Write-Output "✓ N8N_HOST=$env:N8N_HOST"
Write-Output "✓ N8N_PORT=$env:N8N_PORT"
Write-Output "✓ N8N_PROTOCOL=$env:N8N_PROTOCOL"
Write-Output ""

# Iniciar N8N
Write-Output "Iniciando N8N..."
Write-Output "Acesse: http://localhost:5678"
Write-Output "URL Pública: $env:WEBHOOK_URL"
Write-Output ""
Write-Output "="*80
Write-Output ""

n8n start

# 🧪 TESTE E2E COMPLETO - Docker N8N + Evolution API + Chatwoot

**Arquitetura:** v2.0 (Unificada - 1 Túnel Nginx)

**Data:** 2026-02-09

---

## 📋 Checklist de Testes End-to-End

Este guia garante que TODOS os serviços estão funcionando corretamente.

---

## ✅ FASE 1: Preparação do Ambiente

### 1.1 Verificar Pré-requisitos

```powershell
# Verificar Docker
docker --version
# Esperado: Docker version 24.x ou superior

# Verificar Docker Compose
docker compose version
# Esperado: Docker Compose version v2.x

# Verificar SSH (para túnel Serveo)
ssh -V
# Esperado: OpenSSH_xxx
```

### 1.2 Limpar Túneis Órfãos

```powershell
# Matar todos os túneis Serveo existentes
.\cleanup-tunnels.ps1
```

---

## ✅ FASE 2: Iniciar Serviços Docker

### 2.1 Subir Containers

```powershell
# Iniciar TODOS os serviços
docker compose up -d

# Aguardar 10 segundos para os serviços iniciarem
Start-Sleep -Seconds 10
```

### 2.2 Verificar Status dos Containers

```powershell
# Verificar se todos estão rodando
docker compose ps

# Esperado:
# NAME                  STATUS
# postgres              Up (healthy)
# redis                 Up (healthy)
# lab_evolution_api     Up
# chatwoot_rails        Up
# chatwoot_sidekiq      Up
# n8n                   Up
# nginx_reverse_proxy   Up
# adminer               Up
```

### 2.3 Verificar Logs Iniciais

```powershell
# Ver logs do Nginx (reverse proxy)
docker compose logs nginx | Select-Object -Last 20

# Ver logs do N8N
docker compose logs n8n | Select-Object -Last 20
```

---

## ✅ FASE 3: Testar Serviços Locais

### 3.1 Executar Script de Testes

```powershell
# Script automatizado de testes
.\test-services.ps1

# Esperado: TODOS OS TESTES PASSARAM!
```

### 3.2 Testes Manuais (Opcional)

```powershell
# Test 1: Nginx Reverse Proxy
curl http://localhost:8081/health
# Esperado: 200 healthy

# Test 2: N8N
curl http://localhost:5678
# Esperado: 200 (HTML da interface N8N)

# Test 3: Evolution API
curl http://localhost:8080
# Esperado: {"status": 200, "version": "2.2.3"}

# Test 4: Chatwoot
curl http://localhost:3000
# Esperado: 302 (redirect)

# Test 5: Adminer
curl http://localhost:8082
# Esperado: 200 (página Adminer)
```

---

## ✅ FASE 4: Testar Nginx Reverse Proxy (Path-based)

### 4.1 Testar Rota /health

```powershell
curl http://localhost:8081/health
# Esperado: 200 healthy
```

### 4.2 Testar Rota /n8n/

```powershell
# Via curl
curl http://localhost:8081/n8n/

# Via navegador
http://localhost:8081/n8n/
# Esperado: Interface do N8N (com basic auth)
```

### 4.3 Testar Rota /evo/

```powershell
# Via curl
curl http://localhost:8081/evo/

# Via navegador
http://localhost:8081/evo/
# Esperado: JSON da Evolution API
```

### 4.4 Testar Rota /chat/

```powershell
# Via navegador
http://localhost:8081/chat/
# Esperado: Interface do Chatwoot
```

---

## ✅ FASE 5: Iniciar Túnel Público

### 5.1 Iniciar Túnel Único

```powershell
# Script único de túnel
.\tunnel-all.ps1

# Esperado:
# [✓] TÚNEL ATIVO E FUNCIONANDO!
# URLs Públicas:
#   • N8N:        https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/n8n/
#   • Evolution:  https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/evo/
#   • Chatwoot:   https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/chat/
```

### 5.2 Testar URLs Públicas

```powershell
# Test 1: Nginx Proxy
curl https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/health
# Esperado: 200 healthy

# Test 2: N8N (via túnel)
curl https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/n8n/
# Esperado: 200 (com prompt de basic auth)

# Test 3: Evolution API (via túnel)
curl https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/evo/
# Esperado: {"status": 200, ...}

# Test 4: Chatwoot (via túnel)
curl https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/chat/
# Esperado: 302 ou 200
```

---

## ✅ FASE 6: Testar Integração dos Serviços

### 6.1 Criar Instância Evolution API

```powershell
curl -X POST https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/evo/instance/create `
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" `
  -H "apikey: oxSHDTXVWAxaLqZRXomMPShY93xhmgbzVZwd1QJJ" `
  -d '{
    "instanceName": "teste-e2e",
    "integration": "WHATSAPP-BAILEYS",
    "chatwootAccountId": "1",
    "chatwootToken": "SEU_TOKEN_CHATWOOT",
    "chatwootUrl": "https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/chat",
    "chatwootSignMsg": true
  }'

# Esperado: {"instance": {"name": "teste-e2e", ...}}
```

### 6.2 Verificar Webhook Evolution API

```powershell
curl https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/evo/instance/connect/teste-e2e `
  -H "apikey: oxSHDTXVWAxaLqZRXomMPShY93xhmgbzVZwd1QJJ"

# Esperado: QR Code ou base64
```

### 6.3 Testar N8N Webhook

1. Acessar: https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/n8n/
2. Login: admin / N3wK6O0w005VVpFjaOhF
3. Criar workflow com Webhook
4. Testar webhook público

---

## ✅ FASE 7: Testar Shutdown Limpo

### 7.1 Encerrar Túnel

```powershell
# Pressione Ctrl+C no terminal do tunnel-all.ps1

# Esperado:
# [CLEANUP] Iniciando shutdown limpo...
# [SOCKET] Enviando comando 'exit' via Control Socket...
# [SOCKET] Shutdown limpo enviado com sucesso!
# [✓] Túnel encerrado corretamente
```

### 7.2 Verificar Cleanup

```powershell
# Verificar se não há processos SSH restantes
.\test-list-ssh.ps1

# Esperado: Nenhum processo SSH com 'maceio' encontrado
```

### 7.3 Parar Containers (Opcional)

```powershell
# Parar todos os containers
docker compose down

# Parar e remover volumes (CUIDADO - perde dados!)
# docker compose down -v
```

---

## 📊 Checklist Final

Marque cada item quando completar:

- [ ] Docker e Docker Compose instalados
- [ ] Túneis órfãos limpos
- [ ] Containers iniciados (docker compose up -d)
- [ ] Todos os containers com status "Up"
- [ ] Teste local (.\test-services.ps1) passou
- [ ] Nginx reverse proxy funcionando
- [ ] N8N acessível via localhost:5678
- [ ] Evolution API acessível via localhost:8080
- [ ] Chatwoot acessível via localhost:3000
- [ ] Nginx Proxy funcionando (localhost:8081)
- [ ] Path-based routing funcionando (/n8n/, /evo/, /chat/)
- [ ] Túnel Serveo ativo (.\tunnel-all.ps1)
- [ ] URLs públicas acessíveis
- [ ] Instância Evolution API criada
- [ ] N8N webhooks testados
- [ ] Shutdown limpo funcionando (Ctrl+C)

---

## 🚨 Problemas Comuns

### Containers não iniciam

```powershell
# Ver logs específicos
docker compose logs postgres
docker compose logs redis
docker compose logs n8n

# Verificar portas em uso
netstat -ano | findstr :5678
netstat -ano | findstr :8080
netstat -ano | findstr :8081
```

### N8N não acessível

```powershell
# Verificar database N8N
docker compose exec postgres psql -U postgres -c "\l"

# Se não existir, criar:
docker compose exec postgres psql -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE n8n;"

# Reiniciar N8N
docker compose restart n8n
```

### Chatwoot em branco

```powershell
# Rodar migrations
docker compose exec chatwoot-rails bundle exec rails db:chatwoot_prepare

# Reiniciar
docker compose restart chatwoot-rails chatwoot-sidekiq
```

### Túnel Serveo não funciona

```powershell
# 1. Verificar se Nginx está rodando
curl http://localhost:8081/health

# 2. Limpar túneis órfãos
.\cleanup-tunnels.ps1

# 3. Tentar novamente
.\tunnel-all.ps1
```

---

# 🚀 Docker N8N + Evolution API + Chatwoot (Arquitetura UNIFICADA)

Stack completa de automação com **N8N**, **Evolution API** (WhatsApp) e **Chatwoot** (atendimento).

**NOVO:** Apenas **1 túnel Serveo** para todos os serviços via **Nginx Reverse Proxy**!

**Status:** ✅ Configurado e funcionando

---

## 🎯 Arquitetura

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1 Túnel Serveo ÚNICO (porta 8081 → Nginx)                 │
│  https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                           ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Nginx Reverse Proxy (Docker)                              │
│  Distribui por PATH:                                        │
│    • /n8n/*  → N8N (5678)                                   │
│    • /evo/*  → Evolution API (8080)                         │
│    • /chat/* → Chatwoot (3000)                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                           ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Docker Services                                            │
│  • N8N (imagem oficial n8nio/n8n)                           │
│  • Evolution API v2.2.3                                     │
│  • Chatwoot (Rails + Sidekiq)                               │
│  • PostgreSQL + pgvector                                    │
│  • Redis                                                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 🌐 URLs Públicas (1 Única URL)

| Serviço | URL Pública (com path) |
|---------|------------------------|
| **N8N** | https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/**n8n/** |
| **Evolution API** | https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/**evo/** |
| **Chatwoot** | https://maceio-all-serveo.serveousercontent.com/**chat/** |

**Acesso local (debug):**
- Nginx: http://localhost:8081
- N8N: http://localhost:5678
- Evolution API: http://localhost:8080
- Chatwoot: http://localhost:3000
- Adminer: http://localhost:8082

---

## ⚡ Início Rápido

### 1. Iniciar Docker
```powershell
docker compose up -d
```

### 2. Iniciar Túnel Único (Apenas 1 Terminal!)
```powershell
.\tunnel-all.ps1
```

**✅ Pronto!** Todos os serviços acessíveis via **1 única URL** com paths diferentes.

**Encerrar túnel:** Pressione **Ctrl+C** (shutdown limpo via SSH Control Socket)

## 🛠️ Scripts Disponíveis

| Script | Descrição |
|--------|-----------|
| `tunnel-all.ps1` | **ÚNICO túnel** para todos os serviços via Nginx + **SSH Control Socket** |
| `cleanup-tunnels.ps1` | Matar todos os túneis Serveo de uma vez |

### Scripts de Gerenciamento
| Script | Descrição |
|--------|-----------|
| `stop-all.ps1` | Parar todos os containers |

| Script | Status |
|--------|--------|

| `start-n8n.ps1` | ⚠️ OBSOLETO (N8N agora roda no Docker) |

---

## 📚 Estrutura de Arquivos

### Essenciais (Raiz)
- **`docker-compose.yml`** - Docker com N8N oficial + Nginx + Evolution + Chatwoot
- **`.env`** - Variáveis de ambiente
- **`nginx.conf`** - Configuração do Reverse Proxy (roteamento por path)
- **`tunnel-all.ps1`** - Script único de túnel com SSH Control Socket

### Documentação
- **`README.md`** - Este arquivo
- **`docs/`** - Documentação adicional

---

## 🔧 Como Funciona o Reverse Proxy

### Nginx Configuration (`nginx.conf`)

O Nginx recebe requisições na porta **8081** e distribui por **path**:

```nginx
# N8N: https://domínio/n8n/*
location /n8n/ {
    rewrite ^/n8n/(.*) /$1 break;  # Remove /n8n do path
    proxy_pass http://n8n:5678;    # Container N8N
}

# Evolution API: https://domínio/evo/*
location /evo/ {
    rewrite ^/evo/(.*) /$1 break;
    proxy_pass http://evolution-api:8080;
}

# Chatwoot: https://domínio/chat/*
location /chat/ {
    rewrite ^/chat/(.*) /$1 break;
    proxy_pass http://chatwoot-rails:3000;
}
```

### Vantagens desta Arquitetura

✅ **Apenas 1 túnel SSH** (em vez de 3)
✅ **Apenas 1 terminal** (em vez de 3)
✅ **Apenas 1 URL pública** com paths organizados
✅ **N8N oficial no Docker** (não precisa rodar localmente)
✅ **Shutdown limpo** com SSH Control Socket
✅ **WebSocket suportado** (Evolution, Chatwoot Cable, N8N editor)
✅ **Health checks** para todos os serviços
✅ **Auto-restart** com healthcheck dependencies

## 🔧 Comandos Úteis

# Ver status dos containers
docker compose ps

# Ver logs
docker compose logs -f nginx
docker compose logs -f n8n
docker compose logs -f chatwoot-rails
docker compose logs -f evolution-api

docker compose restart n8n

# Parar tudo

```

## ⚠️ Troubleshooting

### Túnel não funciona (502)
```powershell
# 1. Verificar se Nginx está rodando
curl http://localhost:8081/health

# 2. Verificar logs do Nginx
docker compose logs nginx

# 3. Verificar se containers estão up
docker compose ps
```

### N8N não acessível
```powershell
# Ver logs do N8N
docker compose logs -f n8n

# Verificar se PostgreSQL está ok
docker compose logs postgres

# Verificar database N8N criado
docker compose exec postgres psql -U postgres -c "\l"
```

### Chatwoot não carrega
```powershell
# Reiniciar Chatwoot
docker compose restart chatwoot-rails chatwoot-sidekiq

# Rodar migrations do Chatwoot
docker compose exec chatwoot-rails bundle exec rails db:chatwoot_prepare
```

### Erro "remote port forwarding failed"
**Causa:** Túnel órfão no servidor Serveo

**Solução:**
1. Pressione **Ctrl+C** no script (shutdown limpo via SSH Control Socket)
2. Aguardar 2-3 segundos
3. Executar novamente: `.\tunnel-all.ps1`

Se persistir: `.\cleanup-tunnels.ps1`

### Containers não iniciam
```powershell
# Ver status detalhado
docker compose ps -a

# Ver logs de erro
docker compose logs [servico]

# Verificar rede Docker
docker network ls
docker network inspect docker-n8n-evo_minha_rede
```

---

## 🔄 Migração da Arquitetura Antiga

### O que mudou?

| Antigo (3 túneis) | Novo (1 túnel) |
|------------------|----------------|
| 3 scripts de túnel | 1 script (`tunnel-all.ps1`) |
| 3 terminais | 1 terminal |
| 3 URLs separadas | 1 URL com paths |
| N8N rodando localmente | N8N no Docker (imagem oficial) |
| Sem reverse proxy | Nginx reverse proxy |

### Scripts obsoletos (não usar mais)
- ❌ `tunnel-evolution.ps1`
- ❌ `tunnel-chatwoot.ps1`
- ❌ `tunnel-n8n.ps1`
- ❌ `start-n8n.ps1`

**Status:** ✅ **Configurado e TESTADO (E2E aprovado)**

**📚 Documentação Rápida:** [GUIA-RAPIDO.md](GUIA-RAPIDO.md) | **📋 Testes:** [docs/TESTE-E2E-COMPLETO.md](docs/TESTE-E2E-COMPLETO.md) | **✅ Resultados:** [docs/RESULTADO-TESTE-E2E.md](docs/RESULTADO-TESTE-E2E.md)

                           ↓

│    • /evo/*  → Evolution API (8080)                         │
│    • /chat/* → Chatwoot (3000)                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                           ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Docker Services                                            │
│  • N8N (imagem oficial n8nio/n8n)                           │
│  • Evolution API v2.2.3                                     │
│  • Chatwoot (Rails + Sidekiq)                               │
│  • PostgreSQL + pgvector                                    │
│  • Redis                                                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```
